title: trisetum flavescens subsp. fuscum
intro: Trisetum flavescens subsp. fuscum, also known as Fuscum, is a subspecies of the Trisetum flavescens grass, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a traditional medicinal herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Fuscum include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Fuscum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to make medicinal decoctions and poultices.
